news.headline: Anger grows in Venezuela as citizens blocked from aiding earthquake rescue
news.summary: Anger is mounting in Venezuela after the military barred citizens from entering zones devastated by the earthquakes.

news.headline: Iran war live: Trump threatens Tehran as US bombs Sirik, Qeshm for 2nd day
news.summary: US president threatens to 'militarily complete the job' as the US strikes Sirik and Qeshm Island over ship attacks.

news.headline: US launches second night of strikes on Iran after ship hit by drone
news.summary: The attacks come after an alleged Iranian drone struck another commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday.

news.headline: Videos show Israeli settlers trying to seize house in occupied West Bank
news.summary: Videos show Israeli settlers trying to seize a house under construction in the occupied West Bank.

news.headline: Donald Trump nominates ex-state trooper Lance Schroyer as ICE director
news.summary: The immigration enforcement agency has been without a Senate-confirmed director since 2017, during Trump's first term.

news.headline: Colombia vs Portugal LIVE: FIFA World Cup 2026
news.summary: Follow updates as the top two teams in Group K face off - including the build-up, analysis and live text commentary.

news.headline: Serbia’s President Aleksandar Vucic says will resign within ‘weeks’
news.summary: Vucic is under ⁠pressure after months ⁠of antigovernment protests.

news.headline: Another powerful magnitude 4.8 earthquake hits near Venezuela
news.summary: The latest quake comes as rescuers are still searching for survivors of the twin 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes on Wednesday.

news.headline: DR Congo superfan denied US visa to support team at World Cup
news.summary: Michel Kuka Mboladinga is iconic for his motionless tribute at DRC games, but a US World Cup repeat has been denied.

news.headline: Why is Crimea critical to the Russia–Ukraine war?
news.summary: Ukraine is intensifying attacks on Russian-controlled Crimea as Kyiv tries to pressure Moscow into ending the war.

news.headline: Israel orders troops to prepare for ‘extended stay’ in Lebanon
news.summary: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz says the military has been ordered to prepare for an ‘extended stay’ in Lebanon.

news.headline: US Senate challenger, incumbent with same name to appear on Alaska ballot
news.summary: Republican Senator Dan Sullivan has sought to have a namesake primary challenger removed, citing midterm confusion.

news.headline: South Africa’s Broos smiles and snarls before Canada World Cup showdown
news.summary: South Africa and Canada are both competing in the knockout stages of a World Cup for the first time.

news.headline: Two killed, dozens injured in Israeli strike on displacement tents in Gaza
news.summary: Two killed and dozens injured in Israeli strike on displacement tents in Gaza

news.headline: Kenya rights groups say protesters found dumped, tortured after arrests
news.summary: Six found and one missing, NGOs say, after arrests at memorial gatherings for protesters killed in 2024.

news.headline: Iran urge FIFA to stand up to ‘really terrible’ World Cup treatment by US
news.summary: Iran, who played their three group-stage matches at World Cup 2026, now face a wait to know if they will reach last 32.

news.headline: Australia to double fines on Big Tech as children bypass social media ban
news.summary: Canberra says tech platforms are still letting too many children bypass its under-16 social media ban.

news.headline: How is framework agreement with Lebanon viewed in Israel?
news.summary: Israel, Lebanon and US have signed deal aimed at ending hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah, but group rejects it.

news.headline: Tropical Storm Mekkhala leaves Taiwan underwater
news.summary: Tropical Storm Mekkhala has triggered severe flooding across parts of Taiwan.

news.headline: Three security personnel killed in attack in Pakistan’s Karachi
news.summary: Police say four attackers were also killed after gunmen stormed a Rangers facility in country's commercial capital.

news.headline: Venezuela earthquakes: International rescue teams join the search for survivors as death toll surpasses 1,400
news.summary: More than 2,000 rescue workers from 27 countries have been deployed to Venezuela to locate people trapped under the rubble following the twin earthquakes on Wednesday, in a deployment supported and coordinated by the United Nations.&nbsp;

news.headline: UN envoy urges parties to ‘stay the course’ towards peace in eastern DR Congo
news.summary: The new head of the UN mission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has urged parties to the peace processes for the volatile eastern region to maintain momentum and fully implement their commitments.

news.headline: Sudan: Window is closing to prevent wider escalation in El Obeid
news.summary: A senior United Nations official warned on Friday that the opportunity to prevent a major escalation in the Sudanese city of El Obeid is “rapidly narrowing” as fighting intensifies in and around the North Kordofan state capital.

news.headline: Strait of Hormuz: UN evacuates 2,500 seafarers before attack freezes rescue operation
news.summary: The United Nations’ maritime agency said on Friday that it had successfully evacuated about 2,500 stranded seafarers from the Persian Gulf before suspending the operation, after an attack on a commercial vessel exposed uncertainty over who can guarantee safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

news.headline: Deaths in US immigration custody must be investigated: UN rights chief
news.summary: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk on Friday called for stronger independent oversight of the United States immigration detention system and investigations into all deaths in US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody.

news.headline: Venezuela earthquakes LIVE: Death toll rises amid recovery and rescue efforts
news.summary: Nearly seven million Venezuelans could be affected by this week’s double earthquakes as the death toll rises to at least 235 people amid massive, growing needs and ongoing emergency search and rescue efforts, UN agencies reported on Friday. UN News app users can follow here.

news.headline: Venezuela quake: ‘People still terrified to re-enter what were their homes’
news.summary: Aid agencies on Friday highlighted massive needs across Venezuela caused by a double earthquake disaster that has killed at least 235 people so far, with search and rescue for people trapped under the rubble still the top priority.

news.headline: Security Council extends critical stabilisation force in Syria
news.summary: The 15-member Security Council unanimously adopted a draft resolution on Thursday renewing the mandate of the UN Disengagement Observer Force (UNDOF) for six months.

news.headline: From Lebanon to the Strait of Hormuz, a Middle East hanging on fragile peace talks
news.summary: Since Tuesday, United Nations peacekeepers have detected no airstrikes or new missile fire in southern Lebanon. The lull remains fragile, however, as Israeli drones continue to fly over the area and military operations continue on the ground.

news.headline: UN envoy warns of growing risks in El Obeid as Sudan conflict drags on
news.summary: The United Nations Secretary-General’s Personal Envoy for Sudan voiced alarm on Wednesday over escalating violence in and around the city of El Obeid, warning that further military escalation could put thousands of civilians at risk and deepen Sudan’s already devastating humanitarian crisis.

news.headline: LIVE: From conflict to stability, how the UN is supporting peacebuilding around the world
news.summary: As wars persist in different parts of the world, it’s perhaps easy to lose sight of the many countries that have managed to recover from past violence, sustain peace and prevent conflict. The UN Peacebuilding Fund, which is marking its 20th year of operation in 2026, has contributed to many of these

news.headline: Thousands trapped in southern Ukraine struggle to survive
news.summary: Thousands of civilians trapped in frontline communities in southern Ukraine are facing a deepening humanitarian crisis as access to food, medical care and evacuation routes continues to shrink, the UN Human Rights Monitoring Mission in the country, HMRRU, warned on Wednesday.

news.headline: Europe heatwave breaks records as UN agencies ramp up health warnings
news.summary: As a record-breaking heatwave grips large parts of Europe, the World Meteorological Organization (WMO), national weather services and partners are mobilising heat-health action plans for millions of people facing dangerous temperatures.&nbsp;

news.headline: Venezuela earthquakes LIVE: UN rapidly deploys aid and rescue teams
news.summary: Two deadly earthquakes struck Venezuela less than a minute apart on Wednesday, with magnitudes of 7.2 and 7.5, causing at least 164 deaths and widespread destruction in and around the capital, Caracas as international assistance begins to arrive early Thursday, with UN agencies rapidly deploying aid

news.headline: Venezuela quake disaster: UN urges collective effort to help victims
news.summary: UN teams scrambled on Thursday in support of the international response to the devastating double earthquake disaster in Venezuela, where buildings lie flattened and people are likely still trapped in the capital, Caracas, and beyond.&nbsp;

news.headline: World News in Brief: Students injured in Sudan drone strike, dangerous returns in south Lebanon, celebrating women diplomats
news.summary: Ongoing fighting in the Sudanese city of El Obeid continues to endanger civilians and damage infrastructure, the United Nations said on Wednesday.

news.headline: Young peacebuilders: Funding matters, but so do trust and partnerships
news.summary: Young people working on promoting peace in some of the world’s most fragile and polarized places say financial support remains essential, but money alone will not sustain their efforts to organize youth to participate in peacebuilding.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on children and armed conflict
news.summary: The UN Security Council held on open debate on Thursday focused on strengthening protections for children caught in armed conflict. The Secretary-General's latest report reveals that in 2025, soldiers and Government forces were responsible for more grave violations against children in armed con

news.headline: Stranded Hormuz seafarers begin mass evacuation operation
news.summary: As the UN International Maritime Organization (IMO) released more details of its plan to evacuate more than 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, one mariner caught up in the emergency has described the ever-present fear of coming under attack.

news.headline: Lives at risk in DR Congo as Ebola outbreak continues to outpace response
news.summary: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) continues to spread faster than aid efforts can keep pace, despite significant gains in treatment capacity and growing community engagement, the World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Wednesday.

news.headline: World News in Brief: UN launches Hormuz evacuation plan, UNICEF youth champion killed in Gaza, Lebanon ceasefire ‘largely holding’
news.summary: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) will begin implementing an evacuation plan for more than 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, the UN agency announced on Tuesday.

news.headline: Central African Republic peace gains must be preserved: UN envoy
news.summary: The Central African Republic (CAR) “has made remarkable and tangible progress towards lasting peace and security” in recent years, but security remains fragile in border areas, including with war-torn Sudan, UN Special Representative Valentine Rugwabiza told the Security Council on Tuesday.&nbsp

news.headline: Israel continues to commit genocide, atrocity crimes by deliberately targeting Palestinian children, UN independent commission finds
news.summary: Israeli authorities and security forces have deliberately targeted Palestinian children, resulting in genocide and atrocity crimes in the Gaza Strip and war crimes in the West Bank, a UN independent commission of inquiry said in its latest report released on Tuesday.

news.headline: New Security Council resolution upholds accountability for attacks on peacekeepers
news.summary: The United Nations Security Council unanimously adopted a resolution on Tuesday that calls for accountability for crimes committed against UN peacekeepers.

news.headline: Report details widespread use of sexual violence in Sudan war
news.summary: A new UN human rights report issued on Tuesday details the brutality and scale of conflict-related sexual violence in Sudan since war erupted in April 2023 and its profound, long-term impacts on victims, families and communities.&nbsp;

news.headline: Ebola in DR Congo: first month of outbreak sees record number of cases – UN humanitarians
news.summary: Ebola has been spreading at unprecedented speed in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), bringing risk and fear into people’s daily lives, UN humanitarians said on Tuesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: World News in Brief: Pope Leo urges action on hunger, humanitarian strain deepens in Gaza, families return to Lebanon
news.summary: Pope Leo called on the international community to renew its commitment to tackling hunger and malnutrition, describing access to adequate food as a “fundamental human right” during a visit to the World Food Programme (WFP) headquarters in Rome on Monday.

news.headline: Sudan: Drone attacks endanger civilians and shut down critical services in El Obeid
news.summary: Escalating violence in and around the Sudanese city of El Obeid is putting civilians at increased risk and disrupting essential services, the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Monday.&nbsp;

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Warnings of ‘dangerous cycle of escalation’ in Ukraine
news.summary: Deadly drone and missile attacks in Ukraine over the weekend represented a “dangerous cycle of escalation” in Russia’s full-scale four-year-old war, the Security Council heard on Monday from Mohamed Khaled Khiari, Assistant Secretary-General in the Departments of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs

news.headline: ‘Better future for Syria remains within reach,’ UN Security Council hears
news.summary: Syria has emerged after more than a decade of crisis with “clear signs of progress”, but acute needs remain and efforts must boost support so the country can “move from survival towards recovery”, UN officials told the Security Council on Monday.

news.headline: Global Ebola cases top 1,000 as UN races to reach DR Congo’s most vulnerable
news.summary: As global confirmed Ebola cases reach 1,000, nearly three million children and adolescents are at risk in the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while efforts increase to treat prisoners near the epicentre of the current outbreak, UN agencies warned on Monday.

news.headline: Powering resilience: How Indonesia is building a more secure energy future
news.summary: As countries grapple with energy price shocks, supply disruptions and the growing impacts of climate change, energy security has become a pressing concern.&nbsp;

news.headline: Myanmar: Aid decline compounds suffering amid ongoing military attacks
news.summary: A drop in humanitarian assistance is worsening the suffering of millions of people in Myanmar after five years of conflict-related violence, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Monday.

news.headline: Afghanistan in crisis: Drought, malnutrition and a worsening humanitarian situation
news.summary: Imagine being one of a family of nine and sitting down to a meal of potato peelings and other scraps, boiled up into a soup. This is the harsh reality for many of Afghanistan’s most vulnerable families, forced on them by climate change and drought, widespread malnutrition and increasing restrictions

news.headline: Sudan: Security Council warns of mass atrocity risk in El Obeid
news.summary: The UN Security Council has expressed alarm over reports of substantial military reinforcements by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) around El Obeid in Sudan, warning of the risk of a potential ground offensive on the city.

news.headline: Ebola in DR Congo: One month on, scaled up response remains insufficient
news.summary: One month after the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak was declared in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, case numbers continue to rise.&nbsp;

news.headline: UN welcomes fresh Lebanon ceasefire reports as rights experts urge Iran accountability
news.summary: The United Nations has welcomed reports of a new ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah on Friday while warning that civilians on the ground are still fleeing amid ongoing insecurity.

news.headline: Gaza: Sneeze and you might get shot, warns UNICEF in alert on child killings
news.summary: After another deadly night of clashes in Lebanon, aid agencies issued a new alert for Gaza, where 265 Palestinian children have been killed since a ceasefire was announced in October 2025.

news.headline: For one young refugee in Uganda, basketball is more than a game
news.summary: When 19-year-old Stephane Kulimushi looks around the basketball court in Kampala where he trains young refugees, he sees more than players.

news.headline: DR Congo: Efforts ramp up as Ebola outbreak accelerates beyond borders
news.summary: The spread of Ebola is accelerating in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) amid continued armed violence, posing a grave and growing risk to the region, UN agencies warned on Friday.

news.headline: UN upholds freedom of movement for peacekeepers in Lebanon
news.summary: The United Nations has again called for freedom of movement for its peacekeepers in Lebanon who continue to closely monitor developments in the south of the country, including in the wake of the recent provisional agreement signed by the United States and Iran.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Gaza in the spotlight as dire conditions continue
news.summary: The Security Council debated conditions in Gaza at the request of its 10 elected members amid concern that the territory's humanitarian crisis is being overshadowed by wider regional developments. The meeting took place under a ceasefire that has existed in name since October 2025 – but nearly

news.headline: US-Iran deal: technical work can begin, says atomic energy agency
news.summary: The head of the UN-affiliated atomic energy agency on Thursday welcomed the signing of an initial Iran-US memorandum aimed at ending the war, before proposing “to sit down” with both parties to assist with concrete measures such as verification of Iran’s nuclear programme, a key sticking point.

news.headline: ‘Stop this madness’: Rights chief warns of impending atrocities as militia closes in on El Obeid, Sudan
news.summary: The UN’s top human rights official has issued an urgent warning that an imminent offensive against El Obeid, the capital of Sudan’s North Kordofan state, carries the risk of serious international crimes and threatens to deepen an already catastrophic humanitarian crisis.

news.headline: Lebanon: 12 children killed, maimed daily, despite Hezbollah-Israel truce
news.summary: More than 100 days of war reveals a shattering snapshot of life for civilians in Lebanon amid Israeli strikes and forced displacement, the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) reported on Wednesday.

news.headline: Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines
news.summary: Treatment capacity for the rare and deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola is expanding in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), as the World Health Organization (WHO) warns that community mistrust remains a major challenge to ending the outbreak.

news.headline: UN officials call for urgent action in Yemen to push peace, reduce hunger
news.summary: Top UN officials called for bolstered efforts to stave off rising hunger and foster a path towards permanent peace in Yemen as they briefed the Security Council Tuesday morning.

news.headline: ‘Some question if Ebola is real’: How trust is central in fighting DRC outbreak
news.summary: In Ebola-stricken Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), winning the race against the disease requires earning the community’s trust first and foremost, humanitarians said on Tuesday.

news.headline: A turning point for Haiti? New security force takes fight to powerful gangs
news.summary: Passengers look out of the back of a brightly-coloured shared pick-up style taxi, known locally in Haiti as a “tap tap” as it makes its way along the Boulevard du 15 Octobre in the east of the capital, Port-au-Prince.

news.headline: While world waits for details on Iran-US accord, UN calls for Hormuz aid corridor
news.summary: As representatives of Iran and the United States reportedly prepared to sign a new peace agreement at the end of the week, the UN on Monday stressed the urgent need to open an aid corridor to transit the choked-off Strait of Hormuz and prevent a global hunger crisis.

news.headline: WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation
news.summary: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has praised Uganda’s response to an Ebola outbreak that has spread from neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while warning that continued vigilance and cross-border cooperation will be critical to stopping transmission.

news.headline: Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts
news.summary: As Sudan’s conflict enters a fourth year, civilians are increasingly trapped not only by frontline violence but by fear, disappearance and detention, according to an update by the Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for the Sudan to the 62nd Session of the UN Human Rights Council in Genev

news.headline: Ukraine: Latest Russian attack kills civilians, damages cultural landmark
news.summary: Several civilians were killed and dozens more were injured in the latest wave of overnight attacks in Ukraine that targeted the capital Kyiv, the city of Kharkiv and the country’s history and cultural heritage, the United Nations said on Monday.

news.headline: Guterres welcomes US-Iran peace deal as ‘critical step’ toward ending conflict
news.summary: UN Secretary General António Guterres welcomed on Sunday a new peace deal between the United States and Iran, calling it a “critical step” toward ending the conflict.

news.headline: Refugee turned peacemaker: The young woman leader transforming communities in the Central African Republic
news.summary: In the heart of the Central African Republic (CAR), a nation long marked by conflict, Nina Mireille Yankinon stands as a symbol of resilience, leadership, and hope, dedicating her life to helping communities torn apart by war.&nbsp;

news.headline: Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children
news.summary: The odyssey begins in Indonesia and ends, 15,000 kilometres later, in Kabul, the capital of Afghanistan. Along the way, critical food aid destined for young Afghan students crosses nine countries, over land and sea, skirting geopolitical unrest and conflict zones.

news.headline: Women and girls caught up in Yemen’s ‘forgotten crisis’ bear the heaviest toll as funding falls
news.summary: Yemen remains gripped by one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises, with 22 million – out of a population of 35 million – requiring assistance. Women and girls account for half of those in need, and two-thirds of them are of childbearing age, placing reproductive health at the heart of the emerge

news.headline: More strikes impact Lebanon hospitals as humanitarian situation deteriorates
news.summary: The humanitarian situation in Lebanon is being pushed further into crisis as hostilities continue between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants – and essential services and healthcare are targeted.

news.headline: UN ‘encouraged’ by talk of possible US-Iran ceasefire deal
news.summary: The United Nations continues to monitor developments amid conflicting reports on Friday of a possible ceasefire deal between the United States and Iran, the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General said in New York.

news.headline: DR Congo: Ebola spreads as agencies brace for child victims
news.summary: The deadly Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is continuing to spread with a spike in child infections an increasingly likely scenario in the days ahead, UN agencies said on Friday.

news.headline: Ukraine civilian casualty toll in May highest in four years, UN monitors say
news.summary: More civilians were killed and injured in Ukraine in May than in any other month in the past four years, UN investigators said on Friday in their latest update.

news.headline: ‘Grave concern’ after dozens of women arrested in Afghanistan for dress violations
news.summary: Gender equality agency&nbsp;UN Women is “gravely concerned” by the arrest of at least 30 women in Herat city last weekend in Afghanistan for allegedly violating dress requirements imposed by Taliban authorities.&nbsp;&nbsp;

news.headline: ‘The day never stops’ for aid workers braving missiles and drones in Ukraine
news.summary: As Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine enters its fifth year, conditions in southern Ukraine have rapidly deteriorated. Maintaining humanitarian operations has become an increasingly complex balancing act between enabling aid delivery and managing risk.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: UN officials warn humanitarian toll in Ukraine is worsening
news.summary: The Security Council met on Monday amid a sharp escalation in hostilities across Ukraine, where UN officials warned that the war has reached its deadliest point since Russia’s full-scale invasion in 2022. Briefing members, Rosemary DiCarlo, Under-Secretary-General for political affairs, said recent

news.headline: Afghanistan faces ‘lost generation of talent and potential,’ Security Council hears
news.summary: As the Security Council met on Afghanistan, senior officials and civil society representatives delivered a clear warning Monday: despite relative security under the Taliban, worsening humanitarian conditions, restrictions on women and growing economic pressures are creating a fragile and uncertain f

news.headline: Deadly quake strikes Philippines on first day of school year
news.summary: A powerful earthquake struck the southern Philippine island of Mindanao on Monday morning just as millions of children were returning to school after the summer break.

news.headline: Children are dying as US sanctions push Cuba to the brink, warns UN human rights chief
news.summary: Children are dying because doctors cannot access essential medicines, UN human rights chief&nbsp;Volker Türk&nbsp;said in a&nbsp;stark warning&nbsp;on Monday, calling for the immediate lifting of United States sanctions against the Caribbean nation that were causing “widespread harm”

news.headline: Haiti: Harrowing needs must be met with long-term engagement
news.summary: Escalating gang violence across Haiti has pushed displacement to record levels, deepening an already severe humanitarian crisis and leaving nearly 1.5 million people without a stable place to live, according to new figures released Friday by the UN migration agency, IOM.

news.headline: Killed for speaking up: UN sounds alarm over attacks on environmental defenders
news.summary: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk called on Thursday for greater protection for environmental and land defenders, noting that hundreds worldwide have been killed or detained in recent years.

news.headline: Growing up with sirens: UN child rights envoy on the toll of the Ukraine-Russia war
news.summary: Children in Ukraine have been profoundly impacted by years of war, sheltering in underground schools – or forced to study online – and living with the psychological strain of constant air raid sirens that could spell death for them and their families.

news.headline: Regional health agency issues measles alert for World Cup
news.summary: As World Cup fever rises in the Americas, countries are urged to strengthen measles surveillance and vaccination amid ongoing outbreaks across the region.&nbsp;

news.headline: UN deplores another wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine
news.summary: Overnight attacks in three key cities in Ukraine have left several civilians dead, scores more injured, and homes, hospitals and shops destroyed or damaged, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country said on Tuesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: UN urges world not to forget Rohingya refugees as aid cuts threaten vital support
news.summary: As the world approaches nine years since the mass displacement of Rohingya refugees from Myanmar into Bangladesh, the UN refugee agency (UNHCR) has appealed to the international community not to abandon the 1.2 million refugees living in the country, most of them in camps in Cox’s Bazar.

news.headline: Haiti: UN rights office welcomes new judicial units to tackle gang violence and impunity
news.summary: The UN&nbsp;human rights office, OHCHR,&nbsp;on Tuesday welcomed the opening of two specialised judicial units in Haiti, saying the move marked&nbsp;“a significant step toward tackling widespread impunity in the country.”&nbsp;

news.headline: UN stresses the need for de-escalation in Ukraine war as attacks rise
news.summary: The United Nations continues to warn against the dangerous escalation in the war in Ukraine, a senior official told the Security Council on Monday, underlining the need for restraint and dialogue.&nbsp;

news.headline: Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow
news.summary: Every day in Kharkiv begins with uncertainty: air raid sirens interrupt sleep; missiles strike residential neighbourhoods, industrial sites, and roads. Anxious citizens rush into metro stations during bombardments and children study underground.

news.headline: UN warns Ukraine war risks spiralling ‘out of control’
news.summary: The United Nations on Thursday warned of a dangerous escalation in the war in Ukraine after a wave of large-scale Russian strikes and threats of further attacks, with Secretary-General António Guterres saying “the death spiral must stop.”

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting
news.summary: The UN&nbsp;Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Ukraine, with Secretary-General António Guterres telling ambassadors following what Kyiv describes as the most devastating attack on its capital to date, that “the time for peace is now.” In the early hours of 23-24 May, Russia laun

news.headline: Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro
news.summary: The World Food Programme (WFP) has deplored a Russian attack on a warehouse in Dnipro, Ukraine that destroyed “a significant quantity” of food aid destined for thousands in frontline areas.

news.headline: Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’
news.summary: The overnight Russian attack on Ukraine, which centred on the capital, Kyiv, reportedly involved some 90 missiles, including a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile and 60 drones. The senior UN official in the country called for an end to civilian harm.
